title: Inspecting Data Frames
description: Use functions like head(), tail(), str(), summary(), dim(), names() to explore data frames.

slides.title: First Look 👀
slides.content:
When you get a new data frame, you need to understand its structure and content. R provides several useful functions:

- `head(df)` – first 6 rows
- `tail(df)` – last 6 rows
- `str(df)` – structure (columns, types, first values)
- `summary(df)` – summary statistics for each column
- `dim(df)` – dimensions (rows, columns)
- `nrow(df)` / `ncol(df)` – number of rows/columns
- `names(df)` or `colnames(df)` – column names
- `rownames(df)` – row names

slides.title: Inspecting a Data Frame
slides.content: Run these on a built-in dataset:
slides.codeSnippet:
data(iris)
head(iris)
str(iris)
summary(iris)
dim(iris)
names(iris)

quizData.question: Which function shows the first few rows of a data frame?
quizData.explanation: head() displays the first 6 rows by default.

slides.title: More Detail: str() and summary()
slides.content:
`str()` gives you the data type of each column and a preview.
`summary()` gives min, max, quartiles for numerics, and counts for factors/characters.

For logical columns, summary gives counts of TRUE/FALSE.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Use `glimpse(df)` from the `dplyr` package for a tidy alternative to `str()`.
